evals.name: api-v1-violations
evals.prompt:
I'm preparing to open a PR on the current branch. Please review the diff below against `.specify/memory/constitution.md` (v1.0.0) and tell me if it's PR-ready. The diff is the actual `git diff main...HEAD` output for this branch â€” treat it as the source of truth instead of running git yourself.

```diff
<DIFF_CONTENT>
```
evals.expected_output: Identifies (a) breaking change to /api/v1/users response shape (rename idâ†’user_id, usernameâ†’user_name, created_atâ†’createdAt, drop updated_at) violating Principle I; (b) log lines exposing session token and password violating Principle V; (c) the email-uniqueness bypass for a hardcoded admin email as a Principle II concern. Flags missing integration test coverage as Principle III concern. Marks PR-ready: no.

assertions.text: Flags the response payload rename as a Principle I (API v1 contract) violation
assertions.evidence_hint: Should mention idâ†’user_id, usernameâ†’user_name, created_atâ†’createdAt, or dropped updated_at

assertions.text: Flags the console.log of session_id or password as a Principle V (secrets) violation
assertions.evidence_hint: Should call out the leaked session/cookie/password log

assertions.text: Notes the hardcoded admin@nutri.test bypass in models/user.js as a Principle II concern
assertions.evidence_hint: Should mention bypassed email validation for admin

assertions.text: Notes missing integration test coverage as a Principle III concern
assertions.evidence_hint: Should mention tests/integration/** is missing changes for this critical path

assertions.text: Returns final verdict PR-ready: no (or equivalent)

evals.name: bad-migration
evals.prompt:
I'm preparing to open a PR on the current branch. Please review the diff below against `.specify/memory/constitution.md` (v1.0.0) and tell me if it's PR-ready. The diff is the actual `git diff main...HEAD` output for this branch â€” treat it as the source of truth instead of running git yourself.

```diff
<DIFF_CONTENT>
```
evals.expected_output: Identifies the new migration file lacks `exports.down` violating Principle IV. Notes the not-null+default+update+alter-default-to-null pattern is a blocking/unsafe pattern (Principle IV zero-downtime concern). Flags absence of integration tests for new findOneByPhone as Principle III concern. Marks PR-ready: no.

assertions.text: Flags missing exports.down as a Principle IV (migrations) violation
assertions.evidence_hint: Should explicitly call out the missing rollback function

assertions.text: Flags the blocking pattern (notNull + default + UPDATE + alterColumn) as a Principle IV zero-downtime concern
assertions.evidence_hint: Should mention table rewrite / blocking lock / zero-downtime

assertions.text: Notes missing integration test for findOneByPhone as a Principle III concern
assertions.evidence_hint: Should mention lack of test coverage for the new model method

assertions.text: Returns final verdict PR-ready: no (or equivalent)

evals.name: clean-feature
evals.prompt:
I'm preparing to open a PR on the current branch. Please review the diff below against `.specify/memory/constitution.md` (v1.0.0) and tell me if it's PR-ready. The diff is the actual `git diff main...HEAD` output for this branch â€” treat it as the source of truth instead of running git yourself.

```diff
<DIFF_CONTENT>
```
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the diff is compliant: GET handler uses authentication.injectAuthenticatedUser + authorization.can + authorization.filterOutput (Principle II); integration tests cover both authorized and unauthorized scenarios (Principle III); error response keeps the {name, message, action, status_code} contract (Principle I). No migrations involved. No secret leaks. Marks PR-ready: yes.

assertions.text: Recognizes Principle II compliance via authorization.can + filterOutput
assertions.evidence_hint: Should call out the RBAC pattern as correctly applied

assertions.text: Recognizes Principle III compliance via the two integration test scenarios
assertions.evidence_hint: Should mention the happy-path and denial-path tests

assertions.text: Does NOT invent a Principle I, IV, or V violation
assertions.evidence_hint: Should not flag fabricated breaking changes, migrations, or secret leaks for this diff

assertions.text: Returns final verdict PR-ready: yes (or equivalent)
